steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to make and support a generalization about Merle Oberon's early career. A generalization states a pattern, and support means providing a specific example that illustrates it. The notes show a clear contrast: early roles were nameless and uncredited; later roles were named and credited.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Alf's Button (1930) was released early in Merle Oberon's career, whereas Forever and a Day (1943) came out later.: This only states two chronological facts — it makes no generalization about what her early career was like. ✗
  • Choice B — Merle Oberon wasn't even listed in the credits of some of her early films, such as Alf's Button (1930), where she played a nameless, uncredited role.: This generalizes ("some of her early films" weren't credited) and supports it with a specific example (Alf's Button). ✓
  • Choice C — Alf's Button and Forever and a Day are both films that include Merle Oberon, the first Indian-born actress to be nominated for an Academy Award.: This introduces her Academy Award recognition — irrelevant to a generalization about her early career specifically. ✗
  • Choice D — In Alf's Button (1930), actress Merle Oberon played a nameless, uncredited role.: This reports a single instance without generalizing — it describes one film, not a pattern across her early career. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
